story.asA: photographer with questions about Recipe's legality, privacy, and conversion quality
story.iWant: a comprehensive FAQ section answering common questions
story.soThat: I can make informed decisions about using Recipe and understand its limitations before converting my presets
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create FAQ Document (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5) - Create `docs/faq.md` with complete FAQ structure (Legal, Privacy, Conversion Quality, Technical Questions) - Write legal answer (reverse engineering, fair use, private use recommendation) - Write privacy answer (WASM client-side, zero uploads, user verifiable) - Write accuracy answer (95%+ metrics, validation methods, best practices) - Write format limitations answer (unmappable features, NP3 constraints) - Add technical questions (browsers, offline use, bug reports) - Follow Short Answer → Details → References structure
### Task 2: Link FAQ from Landing Page (AC-1, AC-5) - Update `web/index.html` with FAQ section and link to docs/faq.md - Choose approach: FAQ link with bullet list (simpler) OR embed top 3 questions
### Task 3: Update README.md with FAQ Link (AC-5) - Add FAQ section to README.md with top 3 questions and link to full FAQ
### Task 4: Cross-Reference FAQ with Existing Documentation (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-4) - Verify Legal Disclaimer alignment (Story 7-4) - Verify Privacy Messaging alignment (Story 2-9) - Verify Format Compatibility Matrix alignment (Story 7-2) - Verify Accuracy Metrics alignment (Epic 1 Retrospective)
### Task 5: Add FAQ to Documentation Index (AC-6) - Update `docs/index.md` with FAQ entry (if index exists)
### Task 6: Deploy and Verify FAQ Accessibility - Commit changes to Git - Verify Cloudflare Pages deployment - Test FAQ accessibility from landing page and README - Test mobile-responsive design - Test all external and internal links

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: FAQ Answers "Is this legal? (reverse engineering)" Question clearly addresses legal concerns with answer including: - Reverse engineering context explained (Nikon .np3 undocumented) - Fair use and interoperability exemptions cited - Research and educational purposes (DMCA 1201) - No affiliation with Nikon/Adobe stated - Recommendation: Private use only until legal assessment complete - Honest tone: Not legal advice disclaimer, conservative approach - Links to DMCA 1201 exemptions, Fair use doctrine, Legal disclaimer (Story 7-4)
### AC-2: FAQ Answers "Is my data private?" Question directly addresses privacy concerns with answer including: - Confirms YES, files remain 100% private - WebAssembly architecture: All processing client-side - Zero server uploads, no network requests during conversion - No tracking, no analytics, no cookies, no data collection - Simple technical explanation (WASM runs on user's computer) - Proof: Network monitoring shows zero uploads - Links to Privacy messaging (Story 2-9), Architecture documentation
### AC-3: FAQ Answers "Why doesn't [feature] convert?" Question addresses user confusion with answer including: - Format limitations explained (NP3 simpler than XMP/lrtemplate) - Examples of unmappable features: Tone Curves, Grain, Vignette, Split Toning, Clarity, Dehaze - What DOES convert (95%+ accuracy): Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, HSL (24 parameters), Temperature, Tint, Sharpness - Recipe's behavior: Warns user, converts what it can, skips unmappable features - Links to Format Compatibility Matrix (Story 7-2) for complete parameter list
### AC-4: FAQ Answers "How accurate is conversion?" Question addresses conversion quality with answer including: - 95%+ accuracy target for core adjustments - Accuracy breakdown: Direct mappings (98%+), Approximations (90-95%), Unmappable (N/A) - Validation methods: Round-trip testing, Visual validation, 1,501 sample files tested - What affects accuracy: Direct 1:1 mappings vs approximations vs format limitations - Best practices: Always visually validate, understand limitations, expect 95%+ for core - Links to Format Compatibility Matrix, Epic 1 Retrospective
### AC-5: Answers are Clear and Concise All answers follow structure: - Short Answer First: 1-2 sentence immediate answer - Length: 2-8 paragraphs (not too brief, not overwhelming) - Clear Structure: Headings, tables, bullet lists for scannable content - Non-Technical Language: Avoids jargon, defines technical terms - Actionable: Provides next steps, links to details, recommendations
### AC-6: FAQ Updated Based on User Feedback (Post-Launch) Process defined for continuous improvement: - Monitor GitHub Issues for common questions (3+ times → Add to FAQ) - Draft answer based on existing responses - Review for clarity and accuracy - Update FAQ document - Link to FAQ in future Issue responses - Include "Last Updated" date at top of FAQ

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR-7.3: FAQ - Answer common questions: Is this legal? (reverse engineering), Is my data private? (yes, WASM), Why doesn't [feature] convert? (format limitations), How accurate is conversion? (95%+)

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Users confidently convert proprietary/purchased presets without sharing them. FAQ's privacy answer directly addresses this success criterion by explaining WASM architecture in user-friendly terms.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: AC-1 through AC-6 define FAQ requirements: legal, privacy, accuracy, format limitations, conciseness, update process. All answers 2-4 sentences, non-technical language, links to technical details.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: All conversions happen client-side via WebAssembly. No network requests during conversion. No analytics, no telemetry, no tracking. Files never uploaded to any server. WASM sandbox prevents malicious file attacks.

doc.title: Story 2-9: Privacy Messaging
doc.snippet: 6 Q and A items covering: server uploads (No), tracking (No), WASM explanation (client-side processing), auditability (open source GitHub), analytics (none), browser safety (sandboxed). WebAssembly explained as mini-application running entirely on your computer.

doc.title: Epic 1 Retrospective
doc.snippet: 1,501 sample files tested (22 NP3, 913 XMP, 544 lrtemplate). Round-trip testing validates parameter equality. Visual validation confirms 95%+ accuracy for core adjustments. Direct mappings achieve 98%+ accuracy (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, HSL).

doc.title: Story 7-1: Landing Page
doc.section: AC-5: FAQ Section Linked or Embedded
doc.snippet: FAQ covers 4 minimum questions: Is this legal? Is my data private? Why doesn't [feature] convert? How accurate is conversion? Embedded on landing page (top 3-5 questions) or link to separate FAQ page.

doc.title: Story 7-2: Format Compatibility Matrix
doc.snippet: NP3 does NOT support: Tone Curves (no curve support), Grain (texture effects), Vignette (edge darkening), Split Toning (shadow/highlight tints), Clarity (mid-tone contrast), Dehaze (atmospheric haze). Recipe warns user when parameters can't be mapped.

artifact.reason: Landing page where FAQ link or embed will be added (Story 7-1 created structure, Story 7-3 adds FAQ section)
Story 2-9 implemented privacy FAQ (6 Q and A items). Story 7-3 should reference or extend this content for comprehensive FAQ.
README will receive FAQ section with top 3 questions and link to full FAQ (Task 3)
Documentation index will receive FAQ entry if file exists (Task 5)

dependency.name: None (Go standard library only for core logic)
dependency.purpose: No external dependencies for FAQ documentation - pure markdown content

story-context.constraints:
### Development Constraints 1. **English-Only Documentation**: English language only for MVP (i18n deferred per PRD) 2. **Markdown Format**: All documentation in Markdown (.md files) for simplicity and GitHub rendering 3. **No Build Tools**: Static HTML/CSS, no build step for web interface (vanilla JavaScript) 4. **Conservative Legal Stance**: No legal advice, recommend private use, acknowledge uncertainty 5. **Privacy-First Messaging**: Reinforce WASM architecture, zero tracking, zero uploads
### Architectural Constraints 1. **Progressive Disclosure**: FAQ starts with Short Answer, links to details for deep dives 2. **Cross-Reference Integrity**: FAQ must align with Story 2-9 (Privacy), Story 7-2 (Compatibility), Story 7-4 (Legal Disclaimer) 3. **Mobile Responsive**: All FAQ content must be readable on mobile devices (inherited from Epic 2 Story 2-10) 4. **Accessibility**: Keyboard navigation, screen reader compatible, ARIA attributes
### Content Quality Constraints 1. **Non-Technical Language**: Photographer-friendly, avoid developer jargon 2. **Accuracy**: 95%+ metrics must match PRD targets and Epic 1 validation results 3. **Honesty**: Transparent about limitations, no overpromising on conversion fidelity 4. **Actionable**: Every answer provides next steps or links to supporting documentation
### Integration Constraints 1. **Story 7-1 Dependency**: Landing page structure must exist before FAQ link can be added 2. **Story 2-9 Reference**: Privacy FAQ already exists in web interface, Story 7-3 creates comprehensive docs/faq.md version 3. **Story 7-4 Coordination**: Legal disclaimer will formalize FAQ legal answer (FAQ references future disclaimer) 4. **Cloudflare Pages**: Deployment workflow (Story 7-5) will publish FAQ automatically on push to main
story-context.interfaces:
### User-Facing Interfaces **Landing Page FAQ Section** (`web/index.html`) - Input: User clicks FAQ link in navigation or privacy badge - Output: Smooth scroll to FAQ section or jump to docs/faq.md - Format: HTML anchor link with `href="#faq"` or `href="docs/faq.md"`
**README.md FAQ Section** - Input: Developer/user reads GitHub repository README - Output: Top 3 FAQ questions with links to full FAQ - Format: Markdown with inline questions and `[View Full FAQ →](docs/faq.md)` link **Documentation Index** (`docs/index.md`) - Input: User navigates documentation - Output: FAQ listed in User Documentation section - Format: Markdown list item `- [FAQ](faq.md) - Frequently asked questions`
### Internal Documentation References **Cross-References to Existing Docs:** - Story 2-9 Privacy Messaging: Link from FAQ privacy answer - Story 7-2 Format Compatibility Matrix: Link from FAQ format limitations answer - Story 7-4 Legal Disclaimer: Link from FAQ legal answer (when available) - Epic 1 Retrospective: Cite accuracy validation results in FAQ accuracy answer - Architecture Documentation: Link to WASM security section from FAQ technical questions **External Links:** - DMCA 1201 Exemptions: https://www.copyright.gov/1201/ - Fair Use Doctrine: https://www.copyright.gov/fair-use/ - WebAssembly: https://webassembly.org/ - GitHub Repository: https://github.com/{user}/recipe

tests.standards:
### Testing Standards for Story 7-3 **Manual Testing (Primary Method):** - Content Accuracy: Cross-reference FAQ answers with PRD, Tech Spec, Epic 1 implementation - Link Validation: Click all internal and external links to verify functionality - Readability: Non-technical user comprehension test (ask photographer to read FAQ, verify understanding) - Mobile Responsive: Test on phone/tablet (readable, no horizontal scroll)
**Content Validation:** - Legal Answer: Verify conservative approach (private use recommended, no legal guarantees) - Privacy Answer: Verify WASM architecture explained accurately (zero uploads, client-side) - Accuracy Answer: Verify 95%+ metric matches PRD and Epic 1 validation results - Format Limitations: Verify unmappable features list matches Story 7-2 compatibility matrix **Acceptance Validation:** - All 6 ACs verified (legal, privacy, accuracy, format limitations, conciseness, update process) - Links working (FAQ accessible from landing page, README, docs index) - Non-technical user can understand answers (<2 minutes to find answer)

locations.location: web/index.html (FAQ section or link to be added)
docs/index.md (FAQ entry to be added if file exists)

ideas.idea: **Test Legal Answer Accuracy:** - Verify reverse engineering context explained clearly (Nikon .np3 undocumented) - Verify fair use exemptions cited (DMCA 1201, interoperability) - Verify conservative recommendation (private use only) - Verify "not legal advice" disclaimer present - Verify links to DMCA 1201 and Fair Use working

**Test Privacy Answer Accuracy:** - Verify WASM architecture explained in simple terms - Verify zero uploads confirmed explicitly - Verify user can verify privacy claim (network monitoring suggestion) - Verify links to Story 2-9 privacy messaging and architecture docs working - Ask non-technical user: "Does Recipe upload your files?" (Answer should be "No")

**Test Format Limitations Answer:** - Verify NP3 limitations explained (simpler in-camera format) - Verify unmappable features listed with examples (Tone Curves, Grain, Vignette) - Verify what DOES convert listed (95%+ accuracy parameters) - Verify link to Format Compatibility Matrix (Story 7-2) working - Ask user: "Why doesn't Grain convert?" (Answer should reference NP3 format limitation)

**Test Accuracy Answer Completeness:** - Verify 95%+ accuracy target stated explicitly - Verify accuracy breakdown table present (Direct/Approximations/Unmappable) - Verify validation methods explained (round-trip, visual, 1,501 samples) - Verify best practices recommended (visual validation) - Verify links to compatibility matrix and retrospective working

**Test Answer Clarity and Structure:** - Verify all answers have "Short Answer" section (1-2 sentences) - Verify answer length appropriate (2-8 paragraphs, not overwhelming) - Verify scannable structure (headings, tables, bullet lists) - Verify non-technical language (no unexplained jargon) - Ask non-technical user to read FAQ, verify they understand answers in <30 seconds per question

**Test Post-Launch Update Process:** - Verify process documented for adding new questions from GitHub Issues - Verify "Last Updated" date present at top of FAQ - Verify FAQ maintenance process clear (monitor Issues, add common questions)

**Test Cross-Reference Integrity:** - Verify FAQ legal answer aligns with Story 7-4 legal disclaimer (if complete) - Verify FAQ privacy answer aligns with Story 2-9 privacy messaging - Verify FAQ format limitations align with Story 7-2 compatibility matrix - Verify FAQ accuracy metrics match Epic 1 retrospective validation results - Check for contradictions between FAQ and other documentation

**Test FAQ Accessibility:** - Test FAQ link in landing page (click → jump to FAQ section or docs/faq.md) - Test README FAQ links (click → open docs/faq.md) - Test all external links (DMCA, Fair Use, WebAssembly, GitHub) - Test mobile responsive (FAQ readable on phone, no horizontal scroll) - Test keyboard navigation (all links accessible via Tab key)
